b' Alessandro\xe2\x80\x99s Debut, The Chanel Rumor Mill & Dolce\xe2\x80\x99s Deal
Hi, welcome back to Line Sheet. In today\xe2\x80\x99s issue, news about Dolce & Gabbana\xe2\x80\x99s fundraising efforts, an update on Chanel\xe2\x80\x99s creative director search, and the latest LVMH theories. \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Hi, welcome back to Line Sheet, a Nicole Phelps appreciation email.

In all seriousness, Nicole, who runs Vogue Runway and Vogue Business, deserves more flowers. She gets a few in my new book, Selling Sexy: Victoria\xe2\x80\x99s Secret and the Unraveling of an American Icon , co-authored by Chantal Fernandez . Read all about how Nicole\xe2\x80\x99s journalism changed the trajectory of Victoria\xe2\x80\x99s Secret by preordering the book here . It\xe2\x80\x99s out October 8.

In today\xe2\x80\x99s issue, news about Dolce & Gabbana\xe2\x80\x99s fundraising efforts, an update on Chanel\xe2\x80\x99s creative director search, and the latest LVMH theories. (If you work at LVMH and we don\xe2\x80\x99t know each other, remember: I\xe2\x80\x99m always available via WhatsApp\xe2\x80\x94or Signal if you\xe2\x80\x99re super paranoid!\xe2\x80\x94+1 646-241-3902.) Plus, you\xe2\x80\x99ll find my take on the most important shows of the week so far, from Loewe to Valentino.

\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8 Programming note : Tomorrow on Fashion People , Ssense queen Steff Yotka joins me to talk Paris Fashion Week so far, all the way up to Balenciaga. My report on that show will be in Wednesday\xe2\x80\x99s email, so get a sneak peek by listening here and here .

Mentioned in this issue : Paris Fashion Week, Alessandro Michele , Jonathan Anderson , Chanel, Rick Owens, Daniel Roseberry , Schiaparelli, Nad\xc3\xa8ge Vanh\xc3\xa9e , Herm\xc3\xa8s, McQueen, Se\xc3\xa1n McGirr , Jacopo Venturini , Dolce & Gabbana, Cathy Horyn , Karl Lagerfeld , Julie de Libran , Chitose , Zara, Stefano Pilati , Ralph Lauren , Diego Della Valle , Dior, Delphine Arnault , leather jackets, Jane Pratt , Jun Takahashi , and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR
Considered fashion with a luxury sensibility \xe2\x80\x94 made versatile, accessible, and modern.

Modern Citizen\xe2\x80\x99s new flagship store is now open in New York at 251 Centre St. between Broome and Grand. Enjoy 15% off online or in-store with code LINESHEET .
Five Things You Should Know
  • LVMH sold Off-White to one of those licensing firms : Bluestar Alliance owns Scotch & Soda, Brookstone, and Limited Too. There is so much more to say here (and I will later this week), but essentially this was inevitable, given the reasons that LVMH bought Off-White in the first place. (It was a way of investing further in Virgil Abloh when he was designing Louis Vuitton, then investing in his legacy when he passed.) Most people are probably wondering: What\xe2\x80\x99s going to happen to Off-White? (I think we all know what happens when these firms buy a brand.) I\xe2\x80\x99m wondering: What else is LVMH going to sell?

  • La Dolce deal? : I\xe2\x80\x99m told by multiple people that Dolce & Gabbana did indeed start a fundraising process months ago, led by Rothschild, and that they approached investors as far away as China, including HongShan, the venture capital firm formerly known as Sequoia Capital China, which has stakes in several fashion and fashion-adjacent businesses, such as Ami. (For various reasons, HongShan was spun off into a separate entity from the famous Menlo Park-based V.C. firm earlier this year.)

    The China opportunity is surprising given the blowback that the brand endured, in 2018, over culturally insensitive and ham-fisted advertising in the People\xe2\x80\x99s Republic. (Several retailers pulled Dolce & Gabbana product from their stores.) While most of the fashion industry has forgiven Dolce & Gabbana\xe2\x80\x94or chosen to move past the incident\xe2\x80\x94the bad vibes in China have lingered, and news of the brand\xe2\x80\x99s overtures irked many, which is probably how I found out about it.

    Scandal aside, I hear that HongShan was not interested in the deal because of the very rich valuation. (If you know the actual number, call me.) The Row\xe2\x80\x99s north-of $1.1 billion valuation is going to entice a lot of brands to test the market, but few are as profitable and tightly run. That said, Dolce is a unique property and I\xe2\x80\x99m sure plenty of firms are considering the opportunity.

  • The Chanel speculation is rampant : I heard the funniest rumors this weekend, 99 percent of which will prove to be untrue. At lunch, over a bowl of oeufs brouill\xc3\xa9s , a very clued-in friend said that three people had told her that Jonathan Anderson was already signed at Chanel and set to start in January. (\xe2\x80\x9cHow would that make sense with a non-compete?\xe2\x80\x9d our other friend asked.) Then there\xe2\x80\x99s the Nad\xc3\xa8ge Vanh\xc3\xa9e chatter, which I swear came from a comment Cathy Horyn made last summer that has since spiraled. Another person texted me on Friday that they knew it was Pieter Mulier . Okay! And of course, there is the speculation that the Wertheimer family office invested in The Row because they want the Olsens . Obviously, that\xe2\x80\x99s wrong. The Olsens are busy building the next Chanel, god willing.

    Some people are saying that the Chanel search is a done deal. Others are saying that the Wertheimers have only begun the process. Almost nobody knows for sure. But the volume and intensity of the gossip speaks to a couple larger points. First, the appointment drama is a reminder that mature fashion companies ought to have real succession management. Nothing was stopping Chanel from choosing an heir to Karl Lagerfeld before he died\xe2\x80\x94except perhaps for the fact that the kaiser would have interpreted it, consciously or not, as a symbol of his own mortality. Former global C.E.O. Maureen Chiquet was removed, in part, over her desire to put a plan in place. Instead of so publicly promoting Virginie Viard \xe2\x80\x94who everyone knew would not be a long-term solution\xe2\x80\x94Chanel could have simply released a studio collection for a year or two. The commercial collections are so good, and I\xe2\x80\x99m sure plenty of Chanel customers don\xe2\x80\x99t even know Coco Chanel is dead, let alone Karl.

    Second, the frenzy also reflects the complexity of finding the right person for a historic maison. As one of the smarter people I know said Sunday morning, it\xe2\x80\x99s probably going to end up being someone none of us are even considering\xe2\x80\x94 Julie de Libran , say, or Chitose Abe . (I wonder if the designers whose names never come up in the conversation are annoyed, or if they\xe2\x80\x99re the ones who are actually being considered.) Anyway, in an ideal world, the new appointee will successfully engage with the Chanel haute couture machine\xe2\x80\x94remember, they own a network of specialized ateliers, like Lesage embroidery\xe2\x80\x94 and create a spectacle, \xc3\xa0 la Karl\xe2\x80\x99s supermarche . (I was there! I didn\xe2\x80\x99t take anything.)

    There are very few people who can do both. There\xe2\x80\x99s Marc Jacobs , sure, but he has such a nice life at the moment\xe2\x80\x94two collections a year and a good C.E.O., maybe the best setup LVMH has to offer. Then there is our guy Hedi Slimane , who trolled everyone this weekend by releasing an incredibly Chanel-coded collection for another double-C-logo label: Celine. Those tweed skirt suits and sling-backs looked really fab. But remember that the LVMH golden handcuffs are real, and Bernard Arnault will do a lot to keep talent in-house\xe2\x80\x94everything from doling out loans to increasing base salaries to tasking his own family members with delivering his pleas. Chanel is Chanel, but Arnault is Arnault.

  • Party of the week? : Congrats to Inditex chairperson Marta Ortega and designer-slash-most elegant man alive Stefano Pilati for congregating so many legit fashion people last Thursday night for the launch of Pilati\xe2\x80\x99s capsule collection for Zara, which goes on sale October 3. \xe2\x80\x9cIt was like French Vogue circa Carine exploded in a room,\xe2\x80\x9d was the way one person depicted the candlelit, raw-as-anything space, populated by a lot of famous people just hanging out. ( Kate Moss , Luca Guadagnino , and Charlotte Stockdale were all there, but so was pretty much every designer, journalist, and industry executive.)

    It speaks to the industry\xe2\x80\x99s love for Pilati and the adult desire to have a good time, free of the hangers-on that most of these types of events end up relying on to fill the space. Everyone was there, but you had to be someone to be there. (For what it\xe2\x80\x99s worth, I also heard the Ralph Lauren- Holiday magazine party at Ralph \xe2\x80\x99s on Sunday was super fun. I love when Ralph Lauren goes indie.)

  • Jacob!!! : Popular Fashion People pod guest Jacob Gallagher got the \xe2\x80\x9c Ruth La Ferla job\xe2\x80\x9d on the Styles desk at The New York Times . He starts today. Of course, his job will be nothing like Ruth\xe2\x80\x99s, because the world is very different from when she started filing copy. Obviously, I\xe2\x80\x99m a big fan of Jacob\xe2\x80\x94he is a hard worker, cares deeply about the industry, and is right 50 percent of the time about the collections. He will continue to refuse to discuss his place of employment on any upcoming episodes.
And now to the main event\xe2\x80\xa6
Alessandro\xe2\x80\x99s Valentino Debut & The Jonathan Rumor Mill
The endless, anxiety-filled game of designer musical chairs took a back seat during Paris Fashion Week to a series of fashion-for-fashion\xe2\x80\x99s sake shows at Rick Owens, Jonathan Anderson\xe2\x80\x99s Loewe, Nad\xc3\xa8ge Vanh\xc3\xa9e\xe2\x80\x99s Herm\xc3\xa8s, Se\xc3\xa1n McGirr\xe2\x80\x99s McQueen, and Alessandro Michele\xe2\x80\x99s Valentino debut. And, yes, there was gossip, too.
LAUREN SHERMAN
There\xe2\x80\x99s been a feeling of paranoia in the air during Paris Fashion Week\xe2\x80\x94all the speculation about designers moving houses, a sense among executives that the industry is more cutthroat than ever after two decades of consolidation. This season, however, a few designers have risen to the occasion, and pointed toward a post-luxury future where fashion for fashion\xe2\x80\x99s sake thrives.

On Thursday afternoon, the clouds parted for the Rick Owens show in the marble courtyard below the Palais de Tokyo. There\xe2\x80\x99s a lot of empty talk of inclusivity in fashion, but Owens managed to give the idea substance. He said in the show notes that staging smaller presentations at his home last September had the opposite effect he wanted it to have: \xe2\x80\x9can act of exclusion instead of the observance of respect in the face of our current wars that I had intended.\xe2\x80\x9d This season, he cast students and employees in the show, and all those unique bodies gave the clothes a different tenor. It felt like they were part of the design process, not evocations of some imaginary, perfect human muse who doesn\xe2\x80\x99t exist in real life.

Owens stays above the fray of the designer musical chairs conversation, probably because he\xe2\x80\x99s so fiercely independent. Schiaparelli\xe2\x80\x99s Daniel Roseberry , on the other hand, is very much in the mix, possibly because he\xe2\x80\x99s young, attractive, nice, and has done such a remarkable job giving this long-dormant brand, home to several previous failed revivals, such a special new life. His show on Thursday night was stuffed with clients stuffed in tight skirts, carrying Anatomy bags, and sitting next to a disproportionate number of men in suits. I prefer Roseberry\xe2\x80\x99s couture proposal, and the all surrealist braids-as-accessories trigger my gag reflex, but the casting of the Victoria\xe2\x80\x99s Secret-era supermodels ( Adriana Lima , Candice Swanepoel ) was brilliant and the clothes (especially his sporty take on corsetry) looked sexy in a weird way. That\xe2\x80\x99s what the women in that audience want.

How long will Schiaparelli-owner Diego Della Valle be able to hold on to Roseberry? Probably longer than we think. Building a brand takes time: Jonathan Anderson has been at Loewe for 10 years, and Friday\xe2\x80\x99s epic anniversary show felt like a culmination of everything he has achieved, whether or not it was the final act. Anderson is the most talented designer in the LVMH portfolio, and people want him to run a big house. Was the gold band that was strung on to the invitation some sort of subliminal message that he is committed to Loewe for the long term\xe2\x80\x94a promise ring of sorts? Regardless, he clearly has the support of the company\xe2\x80\x99s top executives. Not only Dior C.E.O. Delphine Arnault \xe2\x80\x94who was there, of course\xe2\x80\x94but also current fashion group boss Sidney Toledano , and his successor-cum-predecessor, Michael Burke , who was wearing a very un-LVMH-executive-like zip-up knitted hoodie. (It looked like The Row, but maybe it was Uniqlo? You never know.)
Anyway, whether or not Anderson is headed to Dior, or Chanel, or Gucci, or nowhere, he is the designer of his generation, which he demonstrated with this light-touch collection, where glistening mini dresses were matched with high-top sneakers, gowns floated off the body, and trouser waists were twisted. \xe2\x80\x9cThose high-top Flow runners will fly off the shelves,\xe2\x80\x9d one avid shopper messaged me.

It\xe2\x80\x99s true, and explains why Anderson is so good. Everything works, from the concept, to the product itself, to the marketing (the ingenious process videos are educating a whole new generation about how fashion gets made), to his celebrity roster, which features every serious-but-not-too-serious actor working today ( Greta Lee , Ayo Edebiri , Josh O\xe2\x80\x99Connor , Daniel Craig , Jeff Goldblum ). He makes it all look so effortless. We know it\xe2\x80\x99s not.
A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR
Considered fashion with a luxury sensibility \xe2\x80\x94 made versatile, accessible, and modern.

Modern Citizen\xe2\x80\x99s new flagship store is now open in New York at 251 Centre St. between Broome and Grand. Enjoy 15% off online or in-store with code LINESHEET .
Fashion\xe2\x80\x99s Long Arc
It\xe2\x80\x99s interesting to compare Anderson\xe2\x80\x99s arc with Herm\xc3\xa8s\xe2\x80\x99s Nad\xc3\xa8ge Vanh\xc3\xa9e , who also recently celebrated a decade in the hot seat and is a designer to watch in this never-ending game of musical chairs. They are two very different houses, with different challenges\xe2\x80\x94Loewe had no position when Anderson started, Herm\xc3\xa8s is forever the hegemon\xe2\x80\x94but it\xe2\x80\x99s still impressive to see how zippy Vanh\xc3\xa9e has made Herm\xc3\xa8s feel. There was an athleticism to this collection: lots of cropped shirts and tank tops, and the bodies she chose to display them on were athletic, too. I loved the boxy practicality of the top-handle Plume, the choice for those who already own every other bag.

Practicality was the name of the game at McQueen, too. What I liked about this collection was the tailoring. We\xe2\x80\x99re seeing a lot of twisted waists this season, and Se\xc3\xa1n McGirr \xe2\x80\x99s jackets cut a fine silhouette. I also thought the staging, with the models walking down a runway reminiscent of a foggy, haunting lagoon, was effective. But I\xe2\x80\x99d like to see something more daring, too. It\xe2\x80\x99s tough to be as inexperienced and scrutinized as McGirr. By comparison, I always think about Raf Simons \xe2\x80\x99 first 10 years in business\xe2\x80\x94he had all that time to develop his themes before landing at Jil Sander.

Perhaps McGirr has the constitution to handle it all, and I imagine the Kering brass are happy to let him take his time, given that they have far bigger fish to fry. ( Fran\xc3\xa7ois-Henri Pinault and his deputy, Francesca Bellettini , frankly looked relaxed in the front row at McQueen as Pinault\xe2\x80\x99s wife, Salma Hayek , snapped photos on her phone.) But fashion is always hungry for a phenomenon, and that\xe2\x80\x99s simply not what this is. If McGirr succeeds in the end, it\xe2\x80\x99s going to be a slow burn. On Sunday morning at the Undercover presentation, where Jun Takahashi \xe2\x80\x99s extraordinary, punk work was on display, someone said that they\xe2\x80\x99d love to see his take on McQueen. I agreed.
Kylie\xe2\x80\x99s Absence & Alessandro\xe2\x80\x99s Debut
Atlein designer Antonin Tron \xe2\x80\x99s big, body-conscious idea is coming along nicely, without the same blinding spotlight. This week, Tron\xe2\x80\x99s exposure was heightened because of a collaboration with Kylie Jenner \xe2\x80\x99s Khy\xe2\x80\x94a great get for a fledgling French brand that could use more name recognition in the U.S. Unfortunately, Jenner didn\xe2\x80\x99t make it to the runway show, even though it was clear she was meant to be there. And yet, while a Jenner appearance is a nice-to-have, the wrapped dresses and floating trousers didn\xe2\x80\x99t need her.

The weekend had a very happy ending, thanks to Alessandro Michele \xe2\x80\x99s first Valentino collection. Truly, congrats to everyone involved. The thing that always bugged me about Michele\xe2\x80\x99s Gucci was that it felt undisciplined. This collection had the same amount of frivolity (his word, not mine), but the Valentino archives gave it a framework. Also, the unmatched quality of the atelier\xe2\x80\x99s work was so obvious, even sitting in the dimly lit room, with every piece of furniture covered in a bedsheet, Miss Havisham-style.
During the press conference afterward, where Michele wore his requisite Techno is My Boyfriend baseball cap while sitting on a makeshift throne, he mentioned that he was in \xe2\x80\x9csomebody else\xe2\x80\x99s house,\xe2\x80\x9d referring to Valentino Garavani . You could see Garavani in the sculpted, accordion-pleated ruffle collars, the bow-topped leather pumps, the quilted calico, and the sprays of polka dots. But there is something more feminine about Michele\xe2\x80\x99s Valentino versus his Gucci; it feels sweeter. Will his fans, beyond the Jared Leto s and Harry Styles es and Karen Elson s of the world\xe2\x80\x94who all came out for it\xe2\x80\x94succumb?

Like I said, it\xe2\x80\x99s harder than ever to justify these types of purchases. Michele is lucky in that he has one of the world\xe2\x80\x99s greatest merchandisers, Jacopo Venturini , as his C.E.O. The lyrics of the song that played through the show were \xe2\x80\x9cjoy has to flow,\xe2\x80\x9d and that\xe2\x80\x99s exactly what needs to happen to get people to buy.
What I\xe2\x80\x99m Reading\xe2\x80\xa6
My idol Jane Pratt is back and we are all so lucky. [Read the Styles profile and subscribe to her new Thing ]

Everyone is wearing leather jackets this week. This one from The Row , this one from Hommegirls , this one from Bally , and this one from Auralee , and of course, the source material\xe2\x80\x94 all the Phoebe ones \xe2\x80\x94are in rotation. Becky Malinsky did a great newsletter about how to wear a leather jacket if you\xe2\x80\x99re not a leather jacket person. [ 5 Things You Should Buy ]

The one styling trick you need is in this look book. [ The Row ]

There was a big Gap Inc. executive reunion this past week and everyone I know who went said it was awesome. Maureen Chiquet, Paul Price , Marie Rao , and, of course, Mickey Drexler , showed up. [ Instagram ]

Sundberg did a fantastic survey of her reader\xe2\x80\x99s shopping habits. It also mentions me a fair amount. Instead of being all self-deprecating here, I\xe2\x80\x99m just going to say thank you. [ Feed Me ]

The Christian Louboutin show was like a David LaChapelle magazine spread come to life and looked awesome. I cannot be everywhere\xe2\x80\x94I went to the CFDA, BFC, and Hadida family-hosted shabbat dinner at Leclaireur instead\xe2\x80\x94but I\xe2\x80\x99m sad I missed this! [ TikTok ]

Anyone who was at BoF Voices in 2018 and remembers the fiber guy knows that Casey is correct. [ Twitter ]

Huge thanks to Hillary , No. 1 recommender, for suggesting Selling Sexy and also Line Sheet. [ Hi Everyone with Hillary Kerr ]
And finally \xe2\x80\xa6 if you\xe2\x80\x99re not skipping Paris Fashion Week events to go back to your room and watch Nobody Wants This , what are you doing with your life?

And finally, finally\xe2\x80\xa6 you should know that we\xe2\x80\x99re messing around with affiliate links. Because why not?

Until Wednesday,
Lauren
